data.title: Tesla's Cybercab, Optimus output to start 'agonizingly slow', ramp up later, Musk says
data.description:
Jan 20 (Reuters) - Tesla CEO Elon Musk said on Tuesday that early production rate of the company's Cybercab robotaxi and its humanoid robot Optimus will be "agonizingly slow" before accelerating over time.

Much of Tesla's $1.39 trillion valuation hinges ŌĆīon investor expectations for its self-driving technology and humanoid robots, even as the company's core ŌĆŗrevenue and profit continue to come from electric vehicle sales.

Responding ŌĆŹto a post on X about Cybercab ŌüĀproduction beginning in less ŌüĀthan 100 days, Musk said the pace of the ramp depends on complexity, ŌĆīnoting that production speed is inversely proportional ŌĆŗto the number of new parts and manufacturing steps involved.

"For Cybercab and Optimus, almost everything is new, ŌüĀso the early production rate ŌĆŹwill be ŌĆŗagonizingly slow, but eventually end up being insanely fast," Musk wrote.

A Cybercab is a two-seater vehicle designed without manual controls, ŌĆŹincluding a steering wheel or pedals.

The EV maker has said it was on track to start volume production of Cybercab in 2026, with Optimus output "hopefully" starting towards the end of the year.

In December, Musk said Tesla was testing robotaxis ŌĆŗwithout safety ŌĆŹmonitors in the front passenger seat.

Last year, Tesla launched a limited robotaxi service in Austin, Texas, using its ŌĆŗModel Y SUVs equipped with a version of its Full Self-Driving software. Early operations were geo-fenced and included a human safety monitor in the passenger seat.

Musk has described the humanoid robot project as central to Tesla's long-term strategy, saying it could eventually dwarf its vehicle business. He ŌĆŗhas argued the robots could unlock massive new economic value by taking on a wide range of tasks that humans are unwilling to perform.

(Reporting by Juby ŌĆŹBabu in Mexico City; Editing by Peter Henderson and Sherry Jacob-Phillips)

data.title: Rio Tinto's fourth-quarter iron ore, copper output beat estimates
data.description:
MELBOURNE, Jan 21 (Reuters) - Rio Tinto beat expectations on Wednesday for quarterly iron ore and copper production in a strong showing for its newly minted CEO, and looked set to retain its crown as the world's top producer of the key steelmaking ingredient.

The production report highlights the operational improvements underway at Rio Tinto as Simon Trott took the helm of the company, ŌĆīwhich is in merger talks with Glencore ( GLCNF ) to form the world's biggest miner.

Part of any merger will have Glencore ( GLCNF ) relying on Rio Tinto's operational acumen to ŌĆŗimprove its mining outcomes. Under UK takeover rules, Rio Tinto has until February 5 to make a formal ŌĆŹoffer for Glencore ( GLCNF ) or say it will not proceed.

"A solid quarter, they have ŌüĀpretty much beat across the ŌüĀboard in the main businesses of iron ore, copper, aluminium," said analyst Glyn Lawcock of Barrenjoey.

"The only thing I'd call out was the pricing ŌĆīin iron ore was weaker than BHP year on year ŌĆŗand sequentially," he said. "The key for Simon in my mind, is delivery on what he promised us in December which is the cost-out program, and hopefully we will get more information ŌüĀfrom them in February."

Rio Tinto's realised iron ore prices ŌĆŹin the December ŌĆŗhalf were up 1% sequentially compared to BHP's 2% price growth over the same period despite issues with China's state buyer, Lawcock said.

BHP said on Tuesday it had accepted lower prices for some ŌĆŹiron ore sales while it negotiates a 2026 supply deal with China.

Rio said it will release the unit cost performance for 2025 and the 2026 forecast with its full-year results on February 19. Shares rose 1%.

RIO VS VALE

Despite the comeback performance, Rio Tinto is still in danger of losing the top producer spot to Brazil's Vale, which reports on Monday.

Rio shipped 326.2 million metric tons of iron ore from its Pilbara operations in 2025, landing at ŌĆŗthe lower ŌĆŹend of its forecast range of 323 to 338 million tons. Total production for the year, including from its Canadian operations, came in at 336.6 million tons.

Vale said in October it was tracking ŌĆŗtowards the upper end of its forecast range at 325 to 335 million tons.

The Anglo-Australian miner shipped 91.3 million tons of iron ore from its Pilbara operations for the three months ended December 31, compared with 85.7 million tons shipped in the same period of last year and exceeding Visible Alpha's consensus estimate of 88.2 million tons.

Mined copper production on a consolidated basis grew 5% to 240,000 tons in the quarter, higher than Visible Alpha's consensus estimate of 214,400 tons.

Annual copper production grew 11% ŌĆŗto 883,000 tons, coming higher than the top end of Rio's forecast range, driven by the underground ramp-up, higher grades and recovery rates at its Oyu Tolgoi mine in Mongolia.

A tie-up with Glencore ( GLCNF ) would buttress Rio's copper portfolio as surging demand from artificial intelligence and energy transition ŌĆŹboosts the appeal of the highly conductive metal.
